Description: Choose topics that matters to you, but keep some healthy distance. If the subject is too personal, it may blur your perspective and make it harder to design with objectivity. Aim for a subject that sparks your curiosity, connects with your values, and challenges you intellectually. Remember: /  / Make it personal, but not too personal → Passion helps, but too much involvement can cloud judgment. /  / Go for AWESOME → Pick a subject big enough to inspire bold, creative solutions.

Crazy 8 is a way of pressure cooking ideas: in 8 minutes, you’ll have to find 8 different ideas for your problem statement. The goal is not to find your final solution but discover the creative potential of your problem statement and strategic axes.

Tache: List the experts for your project

Description: Translate your subject into a precise challenge to solve. Phrase it as a problem statement that shows why it matters.
Collecting user testimonials, verbatim quotes, statistics, author citations, and experience maps to understand user perspectives.
Reviewing essential regulations and standards required to ensure the project’s feasibility and compliance.

Tache: Write an interview guide for your experts

Description: Once you have defined your strategic axes, you can start to imagine who your end-user will be and create personas. This will help you a lot for phase 2, because you’re going to conduct user research based on your personas.

Tache: Create the prototype for your product
Description: Name, moodboard, brand
How do people discover your solution? How do they share it with others? How do secondary users interact with your product? Etc.

A UX blueprint is a visual map of all interactions and touchpoints in the user journey. Without it, features may be developed in isolation, creating gaps or inconsistencies. It ensures a shared understanding and helps align design, development, and business teams.

User scenarios describe how a person might use a product or service in a specific context. Without scenarios, design decisions risk being abstract and disconnected from reality. They make use cases concrete and highlight where friction or opportunities appear. /

• One developed concept with prototype. / • Defined art direction / UI (identity, look & feel). / • Final presentation slides / PDF. / + Portfolio presentation /  / • Final package: PDF presentation + sources (.zip) + project fiche on Neocampus.
